name: Terminal Emulator
description: Interactive terminal input/output area with command history, tab completion, and ANSI color support.

standard.description: Scrollable output with input line and command prompt

slots.output: Scrollable command output with ANSI color support
slots.input: Command input with $ prompt and blinking cursor
slots.history: Command history accessible via up/down arrows

readonly.description: Output only display, no input capability

split.description: Input and output in separate panes

slots.output: Scrollable command output with ANSI color support
slots.input: Command input with prompt and cursor

visual_brief: Dark-background panel with a scrollable output area showing previous command-output pairs in monospace green-on-dark text. ANSI color codes render as styled spans (red errors, yellow warnings, blue info, etc.). At the bottom, a command input line shows a prompt character ($ or >) followed by a blinking cursor. Tab completion suggestions appear as a horizontal row above the input. Command history is navigable with up/down arrows. The readonly preset disables the input and shows only output. The split preset divides into two side-by-side terminal panels.

responsive.mobile: Terminal takes full width. Font size adjusts for readability on small screens. Tab completion uses a scrollable row. Command history via swipe gestures.
responsive.tablet: Standard terminal proportions. Comfortable font size. Tab completion visible.
responsive.desktop: Full terminal with generous width for long output lines. Split preset available. Tab completion and history fully functional.

layout_hints.io_priority: Output history should remain visually dominant, with the prompt/input line clearly anchored as the next action. Tab completion and history controls support the loop but should not break it.
layout_hints.terminal_continuity: Command, output, and prompt should read as one continuous session rather than separate cards or widgets.
layout_hints.split_variant: When split mode is used, preserve one clearly active pane so users understand where command focus currently lives.

motion.micro: Prompt focus, completions, and active pane state may brighten subtly to confirm input readiness.
motion.transitions: Command execution and output streaming should feel like continuous terminal flow rather than chunked UI animation.

keyboard: Focus should move predictably between output, prompt, history, and completion suggestions without losing command context.
announcements: Prompt readiness, command output, errors, and active completions should be exposed semantically so the terminal remains usable beyond color and cursor cues.
